feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Basis - What are the programming skills needed in BTP and Hyperscaler automation world ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Due to the constantly evolving SAP world and changing concepts & tools in the Basis domain area.

What are all the programming language skill sets needed to excel in the BTP AI Gen and Hyperscaler (AWS, Azure, GCP) automation fields for a Basis person ? . OS Shell Scripting and Interface programming are becoming part of the Basis in reality.

Please provide the Programming language details.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: BTP Cockpit (trial) - Integration Suite does not exist or is unavailable
entry.content:
Hi, I want to pass the second exercise Developing with SAP Integration Suite (Task 2: Log in to the SAP Integration Suite). After logging, I could not find Integration Suite, I saw only SAP Business Application Studio. So i decided to add application by pressing create button and... i did it. Now i have Integration Suite on my list with status Subscribed, but when I am trying choose it, i have information: "You don't have access to this page". 
I was waiting few minutes, I was trying to log in again, but it did not help.

What can i do now?

entry.title: SAP CI integration with Cloud ALM monitoring
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

we will be using SAP Cloud ALM in the future (as successor for Solution Manager), among other tasks also for system and interface monitoring. Could Please guide about approach to integrate Integration Suite with Cloud ALM in order to monitor Integration Suite using Cloud ALM,including the main technical steps needed. How would you plan the implementation, which stakeholders would you involve and which challenges do you see? Please also give an outlook for additional ideas which you see as worthwhile for improving Integration Suite monitoring and operations.

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: POST Request Working in Postman but Failing in CPI With CSRF Token Invalid — Need Help
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m working on an integration where I need to perform a POST call to an SAP OData V4 service using CSRF token protection.
 
The strange part is:

✅ The entire sequence (Fetch Token → Use Token → POST) works perfectly in Postman

But…

❌ The same sequence fails inside SAP CPI

even though CPI is sending the same headers.

I will attach the Postman screenshots here:

Below you can see the full end to end process of integration flow :

 

I am facing a persistent CSRF token validation issue while implementing a POST service call from SAP CPI (Cloud Integration). Even though I am successfully fetching the CSRF token via a GET request, the subsequent POST request returns the following error:
{
  "error": {
    "code": "/IWBEP/CM_V4H_RUN/042",
    "message": "CSRF token is invalid",
    "@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "CSRF_Token_Missing"
  }
}

 

End-to-End Scenario

Step 1 — Postman Calls CPI (HTTPS Adapter)

Postman sends a request to CPI with the following header:

X-CSRF-Token: Fetch

This header is correctly received in CPI runtime.

I am sharing my complete flow and steps hoping someone can help me identify what I am missing.
 

Step 2 — Store Incoming Fetch Header in CPI

I use a Content Modifier immediately after the Start event to store the incoming header value.

Step 3 — Log All Incoming Headers (Groovy Script)

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) {
    
    StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder()
    def headers = message.getHeaders()
    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
    
    headers.each { key, value -> builder << "${key}=${value}\n" }
    messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Incoming message headers", builder.toString(), "text/plain")
    
    return message

}
​

 

This helps ensure CPI receives the X-CSRF-Token: Fetch correctly.


Step 4 — Request-Reply (GET) to Fetch CSRF Token

I perform the GET request to SAP backend with:

X-CSRF-Token: Fetch

The GET successfully returns the CSRF token and cookies through response headers.

 

Step 5 — Store Response Headers in Another Content Modifier

I then store the CSRF token and cookies returned by the backend GET request.

Step 6 — Use Stored CSRF Token & Payload to post the data

In the final step, I invoke another Request-Reply (POST) where I pass:

X-CSRF-Token: <token from GET response>
Cookie: <cookies from GET response>

 

 


Issue: POST Request Fails With Invalid CSRF Token

Even though the GET request successfully returns a token, the POST request always fails with:

CSRF token is invalid

or

CSRF_Token_Missing

What I Have Verified

✔ GET request returns a valid CSRF token
✔ Cookies are returned in the GET response
✔ Token and cookies are saved in content modifier
✔ POST request is sending saved token & cookies
✔ Logging confirms the correct headers
✔ No special casing in payload
✔ OData V4 service is working fine in Postman

But still the POST fails.

My CPI i-Flow Structure

Below is the full i-Flow layout I am using:

CPI helper i-flow :

 

 

Request to the Community

Could you please help validate:

  1. Whether my approach of storing token/cookie in content modifiers is correct?
  2. Do I need to combine multiple Set-Cookie headers into a single Cookie header manually?
  3. Are there any OData V4 specific restrictions for CSRF in SAP systems?
  4. Should token & cookie be stored using message properties instead of headers?
  5. Do I need a script to explicitly forward all backend cookies?
  6. Is there any known issue where CPI drops certain cookie attributes (Secure, Http Only, Path)?

Any guidance, correction, or recommended best practice would be extremely helpful.

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
I am having an SAP Trail Account I want to access the SAP Event Mesh and create a small task, which will help me understand how the event mesh actually works, but I don't think trail accounts have the Event Mesh capability right?

I think event mesh was included in the trail account until last year or so, but right now I don't think trail accounts include event mesh service anymore right?

Please help me clarify this doubt, if I can access SAP's event mesh service on trail accounts or not

entry.title: What is the simplest, step‑by‑step prepackaged approach to integrate SAP Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4
entry.content:
What is the simplest, step‑by‑step prepackaged approach to integrate SAP Ariba Sourcing with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud using SAP Integration Suite? Specifically:

  1. Is there a single, clear document that explains the end‑to‑end setup (prerequisites, iFlows, connectivity, key mapping, and testing) for the prepackaged solution?
  2. Can this integration be implemented without SAP Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG), or is CIG required?
  3. If CIG is required, do we need a separate license for CIG, and where is this licensing documented?
  4. Are there any differences in steps or components when integrating to S/4HANA Private Cloud vs. S/4HANA Public Cloud?

entry.title: ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: How to configure Resource Path in OData Sender Adapter
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on an OData to JDBC integration where we are trying to fetch IDoc status information from SAP and update it on the client side.

This is a CDS view with parameters exposed as ODATA which we are trying to consume from ODATA Sender channel.
We have created an HTTP Destination as shown below:

Destination Name: ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS
URL:
https://agvdwdci01.corp.mooginc.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS_CDS?sap-client=250&sap-language=EN

Resource Path : ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set
OData Sender Adapter and OData structure snapshots are attached for reference.

However, we are encountering the following error in the PO channel:

ODATA_CALLING_ERROR: OData call failed
404 : NOT_FOUND [ZCAD_I_IDOC_STATUS(p_days=7)/Set]

The same destination works correctly when tested directly in the browser as well as via Postman.

Could someone please advise what the correct Resource Path should be in the OData channel configuration to make this work successfully?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Unable to See the Left Navigation Integration Suite
Trading Partner Management: Error reading number range ICN_UN_EDIFACT
entry.content:
Hello Collegues,

I'm trying to text the stantard SAP CPI Iflow - Step 2 - Interchange Processing Flow V2 - for SAP TPM - SAP Integration Advisor and always failed in this step:

Error: javax.script.ScriptException: java.lang.Exception: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Error reading number range ICN_UN_EDIFACT @ line 46 in setControlNumbers.groovy, cause: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Error reading number range ICN_UN_EDIFACT

Than I check the configurarion of the partner in the SAP TPM:

SAP CPI number range:

Than I decided to replicate the stantard groovy script in another Iflow to test:

Trace:

Number range modifed:

The code basically extracting the main property the type of number range that comes from:

def recInterchangeControlNumberType = properties.get("SAP_EDI_REC_Interchange_Control_Number_Type");

The same code below I tested in another Iflow and works.

 

Calling getAPI NumberRangeConfigurationService.class
def NRCS = ITApiFactory.getApi(NumberRangeConfigurationService.class, null);
Checking if the property SAP_EDI_REC_Interchange_Control_Number_Type is null.
if (recInterchangeControlNumberType == null){
        recInterchangeControlNumberType = "ICN_DEFAULT";
    }
    try {
             // Variable receive if there is something there
 	    recInterchangeControlNumber = NRCS.getNextValuefromNumberRange(recInterchangeControlNumberType, null);
 	   	recGroupControlNumber       = recInterchangeControlNumber;
 	   	recMessageNumber            = recInterchangeControlNumber;
 	  
 	    if (recGroupControlNumberType != null){
 		    recGroupControlNumber = NRCS.getNextValuefromNumberRange(recGroupControlNumberType, null);
 	    }
 		
 	    if (recMessageNumberType != null){
 		    recMessageNumber = NRCS.getNextValuefromNumberRange(recMessageNumberType, null);
 	    }
    } catch(Exception e){
        throw new IllegalStateException("Error reading number range " + recInterchangeControlNumberType);
    }

Before the groovy the property that comes in the stantard iflow:

If you have faced this before, let me know.

Regards,

Viana.

entry.title: SAP CPI: Can we use a JAR which has classes expecting input parameters as file path
entry.content:
Hi,

In CPI, we have a requirement to add a JAR file.

Classes inside jar are making API calls , fetching some key pairs and storing those in a local directory when used on local system. 

Can this jar be used somehow in cloud integration somehow?

Thanks & Regards,

Mayank

entry.title: Need to know SAP recommended for IDOC interface is HTTPS adapter or IDOC adapter
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

 

Are there any sap best practice suggested, when BTP IS needed to connect with S4 Hana for IDOC connection. 

Do we need to use the IDOC adapter or HTTPs adapter to get IDOC from S4 Hana. We need to know about below details. 

      • What features will be missing if we are to use HTTPS rather IDOC adapter
      • Compatibility and EOIO?
      • Justification from SAP, why they recommended this approach

Regards

Ankita S

entry.title: Shopify Admin Access Token Limits & Approach to Generate Offline Token via Developer Dashboard
entry.content:
Background
Shopify has introduced limitations on Admin API access tokens at the store level, and at the same time legacy custom apps are being deprecated (no new legacy apps after Jan 1, 2026).

For integrations such as SAP CPI / SAP BTP / SAP Commerce / SAP Concur, we often need a non-expiring (offline) Admin API access token for backend-to-backend communication.

This post explains a parallel and supported approach to generate an offline Admin API access token using the Shopify Developer Dashboard, instead of relying on store-level custom apps.

Why This Approach?

  • Avoids store-level admin token limitations
  • Works with offline access (non-expiring token)
  • Suitable for SAP integrations (CPI, BTP, ECC, S/4HANA, etc.)
  • Uses Shopify Dev Dashboard apps, not legacy custom apps

Step-by-Step: Generate Offline Admin API Access Token

1.Create App in Shopify Developer Dashboard

  1. Log in to the Shopify Developer Dashboard
  2. Create a new app
  3. Select the target store during app setup

2.Configure App Version & Scopes

  1. Navigate to Versions
  2. Create a new version (example: v0.1)
  3. Select all required Admin API Scopes (permissions)
  4. Copy the scopes into Notepad (you’ll need them later)

3.Enable Legacy Install Flow

This is important for offline token generation.

In the version settings:

Set “Use legacy install flow” = True

4.Set Redirect URL
Under Allowed redirection URL(s), add:

https://localhost/

5.Release the App Version
Click Release

This makes the version active and installable

6. Get Client Credentials
Go to Settings and copy:

Client ID

Client Secret

Generate Admin API Authorization Code
7. Construct Authorization URL
Replace the placeholders with your actual values:

https://{SHOP_NAME}.myshopify.com/admin/oauth/authorize?client_id={CLIENT_ID}&scope={SCOPES}&redirect_uri=https://localhost/&response_type=code


Notes:

{SHOP_NAME} → Shopify store name

{CLIENT_ID} → From Developer Dashboard

{SCOPES} → Paste all scopes copied earlier (comma-separated)


8. Install App & Capture Code
Open the constructed URL in a browser

Click Install

You’ll be redirected to a broken localhost page or redirected page (expected behavior)

In the browser address bar, copy the value after:

code=

Important:

Authorization code expires in 60 seconds

If the page doesn’t load:

Press Enter once

Immediately copy the full URL

Extract the code parameter


Exchange Code for Offline Admin Access Token
9. Call Shopify Token API (POST)
Endpoint:

https://{STORE_NAME}.myshopify.com/admin/oauth/access_token
Headers:

Content-Type: application/json


Body (JSON):

{
"client_id": "CLIENT_ID_FROM_DEV_DASHBOARD",
"client_secret": "CLIENT_SECRET_FROM_DEV_DASHBOARD",
"code": "CODE_FROM_BROWSER"
}
10. Result
Shopify returns an offline Admin API access token

  • Token does NOT expire
  • Can be safely stored and used in SAP integrations

Now you will have Access token where you can use it in header param (X-Shopify-Access-Token) and graphql API ( https://StoreName.myshopify.com/admin/api/2025-01/graphql.json) and graphql query 🙂

Key Takeaways

  • Shopify store-level admin tokens now have limitations
  • Legacy custom apps are being phased out
  • Developer Dashboard apps + legacy install flow provide a reliable workaround
  • This method is ideal for SAP CPI / SAP BTP / backend integrations
  • Offline Admin API token is permanent unless revoked

entry.title: SAP-HULFT Connection
entry.content:
Hello,

I've a requirement where CSV data needs to be pushed from an external application to SAP using HULFT.

I am look for technical guidance and best practise for this integration, particularly at SAP side.

Thanks & regards

Devender

entry.title: Looking for Expert and Community Insights on My SAP Technical Journey
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are all doing well.

I would like to kindly ask for your feedback and professional insights regarding my current learning journey within the SAP Technical domain. My goal is to continuously improve my technical profile and ensure that my learning path aligns well with real-world SAP projects and current market expectations.

Below is a brief overview of my learning direction and the reasoning behind each focus area.


SAP Technical Learning Direction At this stage, my primary focus is on pursuing the SAP Technical track. I am currently concentrating on SAP Basis, aiming to build a strong and solid foundation in SAP system administration, core architecture, and operational concepts.
In parallel, I am expanding my knowledge across several complementary technical areas, as outlined below:


SAP HANA
My objective is to gain a clear and comprehensive understanding of SAP HANA database architecture and how SAP systems interact with the database layer in terms of structure, performance optimization, and system integration.


SAP ABAP
I am working on building a solid understanding of the ABAP stack, with a focus on how development processes are designed and implemented within SAP systems. This helps me better understand system logic, debugging, and collaboration with development teams from a technical perspective.


SAP CPI (Cloud Integration)
My focus includes understanding SAP’s transition from traditional on-premise environments to cloud-based platforms, along with cloud integration concepts such as internal (A2A) and external (B2B) integration scenarios.

Additionally, I have complemented this learning path with the Apache Camel Framework, due to its importance and relevance within SAP integration solutions.


SAP UI5 / Fiori & SAP BTP CAPM
Recently, SAP introduced important updates related to both frontend and backend development models. I have been exploring these updates and following commonly recommended learning resources to better understand the modern SAP development approach, including CAP-based application development.


Learning Approach
Overall, I am intentionally following a structured and methodical learning sequence to build a holistic understanding of SAP Architecture as an integrated enterprise platform, rather than focusing on individual tools or isolated technologies.


Current Learning Path & Courses
Below are the main courses I am currently following (via Udemy):

SAP NetWeaver Fundamentals

SAP Basis Administration

SAP HANA Administration

Apache Camel Framework with Spring Boot

SAP Cloud Integration (CI / CPI), including API Management and Event Mesh

SAP UI5 / Fiori Full Stack with TypeScript / JavaScript using SAP BTP RAPM / CAPM


I would truly appreciate your feedback on whether this learning path is well-structured and aligned with real-world SAP implementations, and whether there are any key areas you would recommend adding, adjusting, or prioritizing differently.

Thank you in advance for your time and valuable insights.

Best regards,

entry.title: Can I send external RFC calls to SAP CPI / Integration Suite gateway?
entry.content:
I want to establish RFC communication between external RFC interface client and S4U backend system via SAP Integration Suite gateway. Is this possible? Is it possible using lirfc32.dll version 7200 for external client or only for SAP Netweaver RFC SDK 7.50?

Thanks, Rainer

entry.title: Producer Event Mesh IFlow
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like for your opinion regarding designing producer's iflow for asynchronous data publishing and consumption. 
The general idea is to have integration flow that will iterate every 15 minutes over the list of datacenters and query Prometheus logs for every datacenter and then publish the data to the event mesh queue. That would be the producer iflow. Then, the consumer iflow would use the event mesh queue as the sender and push the messages to the FAST API application. 

So far I have been focusing on the producer design. It has the following steps:
1. Timer (trigger every 15 minutes)
2. Content Modifier (body with the landscape list)
3. Groovy script that reads the list
4. Iterator splitter (sequentially processing each landscape from the list)
5. Query Prometheus for each landscape id and build the topic property corresponding to the current landscape
6. Request Reply from the query
7. Publish the message to the event mesh queue
8. AMQP receiver adapter to connect to the queue
I would like your humble opinion on this design. I tested it and it works fine, the messages arrive to the queue and I can use Postman or Bruno to consume them. 
This is the setup with one queue and multiple topic subscriptions within the queue corresponding to each landscape id.
What do you think of this setup? I am using SAP Event Mesh and average message size can be from 2-5 MB depending on the size of the datacenter.

Thank you!

entry.title: SM59 Connection Test Error
entry.content:
Hello.

I am getting an error when trying to connect to the AVALA Tax service in SAP BTP using the OAUTH client profile.

SM59 Destinations Type G  (HTTP External):
AVLR_AVATAX_ADDRESS
AVLR_AVATAX_GLOBAL
OAuth Profile : SAP_COM_0249_PREM
Error Observed : Create Failed

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Testing Details:
T-code: SE38
Program Name: OA2C_GENERIC_ACCESS
HTTP Client Selection: SM59 Destination

HTTP Client Selection: URL option

 

 

 

 

Once SAP enables the required connectivity, the SM59 test should work as expected.

I would like to hear the opinions of seniors who can help me solve the above problem.

entry.title: Need a help in CPI Mapping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Need a help in achieving the below as values are coming as expected a step before the final field(Target) currently being Suppressed.

if E1EDK14/QUALF is 015 and E1EDK14/ORGID for values (ZS1, ZS10, ZS2, ZS6, ZS7, ZS8) then Prefix Minus Symbol (-) before E1EDS01/SUMME value
for E1EDS01/SUMID equals 010, 011 and 005 or else E1EDS01/SUMME.

entry.title: Unable to Activate API Management in SAP Integration Suite – Trial Accoun
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am new to SAP BTP and currently exploring SAP Integration Suite using a BTP trial account.

While trying to activate API Management from Integration Suite, I am getting the following error message during activation:

"There is a temporary problem in provisioning your account, please try again by clicking on Activate. In case the problem persists, please create an incident in the component OPU-API-OD-OPS for further assistance."

Since this is a trial account, I wanted to understand:
- Is API Management supported on SAP BTP trial accounts?
- Is this a known limitation for trial tenants?
- Has anyone faced a similar issue and found a workaround for learning purposes?

I understand that creating an SAP incident may not be possible for trial accounts, so I wanted to check with the community if there are any recommended next steps.

Any guidance or clarification would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Functional to CPI integration path
entry.content:
Hello SAP Guru's,

I am a PM consultant with 9 years of experience and looking for transition into CPI  integration. What is the road map/timeline/challenges to achieve this?

Is functional knowledge enough or do I need to learn programming as well to move to CPI?

Regards

Deepak Doddagoudar
